ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general oolong tea beverage class; no specific packaged peach-flavoured less-sugar variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR covers ready-to-drink tea broadly; no peach-flavoured oolong sub-code exists. Closest defensible match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged flavoured oolong tea; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 500 ml bottle of packaged peach oolong tea, the typical single-serve retail size sold in Singapore convenience stores and supermarkets.

nutrient_source.title: Peach Oolong Tea (Less Sugar) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for peach oolong tea (less sugar, packaged), cr_id D09020062. All per-100g values are as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened oolong tea / lightly sweetened RTD tea
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for peach oolong tea (less sugar, packaged). GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for lightly sweetened ready-to-drink tea beverages with similar sugar content (~4–5 g/100 ml). Glycemic load per 500 ml serving calculated as: GI (45) × available carbohydrate per serving (24 g) / 100 = 10.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar in a sweetened packaged beverage; low sodium (4 mg/100 ml) but absence of potassium data and presence of added sugar classify this as an unsuitable sweetened packaged drink for hypertension management.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid this beverage and choose plain unsweetened oolong tea, as even 'less sugar' packaged variants contain added sugar and lack the potassium content needed to support blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) classification: contains 4.2 g added sugar per 100 ml with 0 g dietary fibre, resulting in rapid glycaemic response; a 500 ml bottle delivers 21 g sugar with no fibre buffer.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; all packaged sweetened tea drinks including 'less sugar' variants are classified as SSBs and should be replaced with plain unsweetened brewed oolong tea.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with elevated triglycerides, increased cardiovascular risk, and weight gain; this product contains 4.2 g sugar/100 ml with no protective nutrients (0 g fibre, 0 g fat, no omega-3).
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid packaged sweetened tea beverages and opt for plain unsweetened oolong tea, which provides polyphenols without the added sugar load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; the polyphenol content of a diluted packaged oolong tea beverage is insufficient to provide a clinically meaningful anti-inflammatory effect.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Choose freshly brewed unsweetened oolong tea to obtain polyphenol benefit without the pro-inflammatory effect of added sugar present in this packaged variant.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Peach Oolong Tea (Less Sugar, Packaged) is a ready-to-drink (RTD) commercially produced beverage sold in Singapore and widely available across Southeast Asia. It is made from brewed oolong tea extract combined with artificial or natural peach flavouring and a reduced quantity of added sugar, resulting in a lightly sweet, floral, and fruity drink. The product is packaged in sealed plastic bottles or cans, typically in 500 ml single-serve formats. Per 100 ml, it provides 19 kcal, 4.8 g carbohydrate (of which 4.2 g is sugar), 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g dietary fibre, and 4 mg sodium. Despite the 'less sugar' label, this product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) because it contains added sugar. It is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The oolong tea base contains polyphenols, but the diluted concentration in a packaged RTD format is unlikely to deliver clinically meaningful antioxidant or anti-inflammatory benefit. The beverage is vegan, gluten-free, and halal-suitable. It is commonly consumed as a refreshment at breakfast, lunch, or as an on-the-go drink. Clinically, the absence of fibre and the presence of added sugar mean this beverage raises blood glucose more rapidly than unsweetened tea alternatives. Consumers seeking the health benefits of oolong tea are advised to choose plain brewed unsweetened oolong tea instead.
common_user_queries: Is peach oolong tea less sugar healthy?
How many calories in a bottle of peach oolong tea?
Can diabetics drink peach oolong tea?
Is less sugar packaged tea suitable for hypertension?

Is RTD peach oolong tea an SSB?
Peach oolong tea nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020062) with confidence 0.95 as reported in the input record. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore foods. All core macros and secondary nutrients are as reported by HPB.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database; this is a water-based tea beverage containing no protein by formulation. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database; this is a water-based tea beverage containing no fat by formulation. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB; packaged RTD tea beverages contain no fibre. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this packaged RTD peach oolong tea are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry as of 2026-04-16. GI value of 45 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for lightly sweetened RTD tea beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. The 'keto' dietary tag present in the input record has been removed: with 4.8 g carbohydrate per 100 ml (24 g per 500 ml bottle), this product is not compatible with a ketogenic diet. The 'less sugar' designation refers to a reduced-sugar formulation relative to a standard sweetened variant, but the product still contains added sugar and is classified as an SSB for clinical purposes.
